content.problemStatement:
Given the rating RRR of a person, tell which division he belongs to. The rating range for each division are given below:



Division 111: 2000≤2000 \le2000≤ Rating.




Division 222: 1600≤1600 \le1600≤ Rating <2000\lt 2000<2000.




Division 333: Rating <1600\lt 1600<1600.




Input Format

The first line of the input contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each testcase contains a single line of input, which contains a single integer RRR.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line the answer: 111 if the person belongs to Division 111, 222 if the person belongs to Division 222, and 333 if the person belongs to Division 333.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Since the rating of the person lies in the range [1000,1600)[1000, 1600)[1000,1600), he belongs to Division 333.
Test case 222: Since the rating of the person lies in the range [2000,4500][2000, 4500][2000,4500], he belongs to Division 111.
Test case 333: Since the rating of the person lies in the range [1600,2000)[1600, 2000)[1600,2000), he belongs to Division 222.
